title: bromus erectus subsp. euerectus
intro: Bromus erectus subsp. euerectus, also known as Elymus euerectus, is a subspecies of the Bromus erectus plant, which is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Elymus euerectus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Elymus euerectus is typically prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and can be consumed as a tea or added to herbal remedies in the form of capsules or tablets.
